message:
fix(codegen): Builder flush and keyword escaping for captured vars

- Add gc module functions to ModuleFuncsReturningValues whitelist
  (get_referents, get_referrers, get_objects, isenabled, etc.)
- Fix Zig keyword escaping when passing captured variables to class init
  (e.g., variable named 'test' now properly escaped as @"test")
- Add flushBuilder() in captureExpr to capture builder output correctly
  (fixes gc.isenabled() generating 'true!()' instead of 'true')
- Add flushBuilder() in exprToValue for module function calls

These fixes enable gc tests to compile and make progress on core2 tests.

files.patch:
@@ -118,6 +118,19 @@ const ModuleFuncsReturningValues = std.StaticStringMap(void).initComptime(.{
     .{ "collections.Counter", {} },
     .{ "collections.deque", {} },
     .{ "collections.OrderedDict", {} },
+    // gc module
+    .{ "gc.get_referents", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_referrers", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_objects", {} },
+    .{ "gc.isenabled", {} },
+    .{ "gc.is_tracked", {} },
+    .{ "gc.is_finalized", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_threshold", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_count", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_stats", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_debug", {} },
+    .{ "gc.collect", {} },
+    .{ "gc.get_freeze_count", {} },
 });
